title: Use stream-json output format for claude -p delegation observability
source: claude CLI --output-format stream-json / --include-partial-messages — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; verify with `claude -p --help`
description: delegate.py spawns `claude -p` subprocesses. If it parses final text or plain JSON, it misses real-time tool-use events. Using `--output-format stream-json` (plus `--verbose`/`--include-partial-messages`) yields a live NDJSON event stream of the sub-agent's assistant/tool_use/result messages, which can be forwarded to roro_events for true real-time observability in the Rick Terminal instead of a spinner until completion.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py, add `--output-format stream-json --verbose` to the claude subprocess invocation and read stdout line-by-line. For each JSON event (system/assistant/tool_use/tool_result/result), map to a roro event and emit via roro_events. Track final `result` event for cost/duration/usage. Guard behind a config flag so plain mode still works; handle partial-line buffering on the pipe.
